plan_adherence_notes: The execute commit is scoped exactly to the planned test-only change: it adds one shared success-path breadth test in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests`, enables `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN = 2`, uses explicit `subTest(label=...)` cases for create-market, probability-edit, market-resolve, comment-post, and event-trade, sends one accepted request per route with a distinct agent id, and asserts `assert_rate_limit_headers(..., limit=2, remaining=1)` after each 201 response. No production files changed.
missed_questions: No step-local `.fleet/tasks/bayes-market-u44-execute-step-3-execute-step-2/questions.json` was produced, so there is no direct artifact showing whether any child-step-specific implementation questions were asked or resolved.
reason: Approve. The implementation follows the recorded plan and the plan is well-supported by the research answers and evidence, especially around using a shared breadth loop, `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN = 2`, and distinct agent ids per route. Validation also reran the relevant test surface with `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py'`, which passed (231 tests, OK). The only notable process gap is the missing child `questions.json`, which reduces traceability but does not undermine the correctness or scope of the code change.
